raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX I paid for a conference fee named " XXXX XXXX XXXX  ''. This conference was supposed to take place XXXX of XXXX. It came to the attention of my community around XXXX of XXXX that the person who was running the conference actually had taken all of the money from those of us who paid in advance and gambled it. I attempted to contact the organizer of this event to ask for a refund but he did not respond. Additionally, his phone number has been disconnected and his website has been shut down. 

As soon as I was able to I contacted Barclays ( end of XXXX, early XXXX ) to ask them what to do, I did. I was advised to send all proof that I had of this fraud and to mail to them along with a letter explaining the situation. I did this, and received a response on XX/XX/XXXX stating " unfortunately, we were unable to obtain credit on your behalf because we can only intervene after the specified delivery date has passed. '' I was advised by agent XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX that it would be ok to wait until XXXX and to write again once the event should have happened, but didn't. 

So, I did. I wrote Barclays again on XX/XX/XXXX explaining the situation and received a response from them on XX/XX/XXXX stating that there was " insufficient proof that the merchant has violated the invoice/contract 's terms and conditions '' and that " we are outside of the permitted timeframes to continue this claim through the dispute process. '' I have provided them with as much proof as possible - and upon speaking with agent XXXX ( ID XXXX ) on XX/XX/XXXX even he was able to verify that there was no way to contact XXXX, the merchant. Additionally, I followed their instruction to wait until after the event has passed and now that I have they are telling me that I waited too long. This is incredibly frustrating. Additionally frustrating is that while XXXX was able to verify that there was no way to contact XXXX XXXX XXXX tried to reach out to him and was the one who told me that his line was disconnected ), his advice was still to take this up with the merchant. I am not sure how they can tell me in the same sentence that they are not successful in reaching the merchant and yet expect me to reach him to get a refund.
